name: ElmSyntaxTypeInfer

unions.name: Base10Or16
unions.comment:
Either decimal or hexadecimal.
Used by [`PatternInt`](#Pattern) and [`ExpressionInteger`](#Expression)

unions.name: Expression
cases: { moduleOrigin : Elm.Syntax.ModuleName.ModuleName, qualification : Elm.Syntax.ModuleName.ModuleName, name : String.String }
{ part0 : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression type_) type_, part1 : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression type_) type_ }

{ part0 : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression type_) type_, part1 : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression type_) type_, part2 : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression type_) type_ }
{ record : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression type_) type_, fieldNameRange : Elm.Syntax.Range.Range, fieldName : String.String }

{ operator : { moduleOrigin : Elm.Syntax.ModuleName.ModuleName, symbol : String.String, type_ : type_ }, left : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression type_) type_, right : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression type_) type_ }

{ condition : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression type_) type_, onTrue : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression type_) type_, onFalse : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression type_) type_ }

{ called : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression type_) type_, argument0 : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression type_) type_, argument1Up : List.List (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression type_) type_) }
List.List { range : Elm.Syntax.Range.Range, name : String.String, nameRange : Elm.Syntax.Range.Range, value : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression type_) type_ }

{ recordVariable : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode { moduleOrigin : Elm.Syntax.ModuleName.ModuleName, name : String.String } type_, field0 : { range : Elm.Syntax.Range.Range, name : String.String, nameRange : Elm.Syntax.Range.Range, value : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression type_) type_ }, field1Up : List.List { range : Elm.Syntax.Range.Range, name : String.String, nameRange : Elm.Syntax.Range.Range, value : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression type_) type_ } }

{ parameter0 : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Pattern type_) type_, parameter1Up : List.List (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Pattern type_) type_), result : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression type_) type_ }

{ matchedExpression : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression type_) type_, case0 : { pattern : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Pattern type_) type_, result : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression type_) type_ }, case1Up : List.List { pattern : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Pattern type_) type_, result : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression type_) type_ } }

{ declaration0 : { range : Elm.Syntax.Range.Range, declaration : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.LetDeclaration type_ }, declaration1Up : List.List { range : Elm.Syntax.Range.Range, declaration : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.LetDeclaration type_ }, result : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression type_) type_ }

unions.name: LetDeclaration
cases: { pattern : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Pattern type_) type_, expression : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression type_) type_ }

{ signature : Maybe.Maybe { range : Elm.Syntax.Range.Range, nameRange : Elm.Syntax.Range.Range, annotationType : Elm.Syntax.TypeAnnotation.TypeAnnotation, annotationTypeRange : Elm.Syntax.Range.Range }, nameRange : Elm.Syntax.Range.Range, name : String.String, parameters : List.List (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Pattern type_) type_), result : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression type_) type_, type_ : type_ }

unions.name: Pattern
unions.comment:
Like [`Elm.Syntax.Pattern.Pattern`](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/stil4m/elm-syntax/latest/Elm-Syntax-Pattern#Pattern)
but all its sub-nodes are [`TypedNode`](#TypedNode)s
cases: { pattern : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Pattern type_) type_, variable : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode String.String type_ }
{ part0 : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Pattern type_) type_, part1 : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Pattern type_) type_ }

{ part0 : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Pattern type_) type_, part1 : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Pattern type_) type_, part2 : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Pattern type_) type_ }
{ head : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Pattern type_) type_, tail : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Pattern type_) type_ }

{ moduleOrigin : Elm.Syntax.ModuleName.ModuleName, qualification : Elm.Syntax.ModuleName.ModuleName, name : String.String, values : List.List (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Pattern type_) type_) }

unions.name: Type
unions.comment:
Type information attached to expressions and patterns,
see [`TypedNode`](#TypedNode).

This is different from [`Elm.Syntax.TypeAnnotation.TypeAnnotation`](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/stil4m/elm-syntax/latest/Elm-Syntax-TypeAnnotation#TypeAnnotation)
in that it doesn't contain
information unrelated to type inference like ranges, qualification levels or parens.

unions.name: TypeNotVariable
cases: { moduleOrigin : Elm.Syntax.ModuleName.ModuleName, name : String.String, arguments : List.List (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Type variable) }
{ part0 : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Type variable, part1 : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Type variable }
{ part0 : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Type variable, part1 : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Type variable, part2 : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Type variable }
{ recordVariable : variable, fields : FastDict.Dict String.String (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Type variable) }
{ input : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Type variable, output : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Type variable }

aliases.name: ModuleOriginLookup
aliases.comment:
How to map names and symbols used in a module to their origin module.
Create with [`importsToModuleOriginLookup`](#importsToModuleOriginLookup).

Contains variants, type alias names, choice type names, port names, value/function declaration names
and whether `(|.)` and or `(|=)` are imported from `Parser.Advanced`.

Also contains locally declared names when available.

Type- and expression/pattern-level members
are separated since you can for example have a local `List` variant
while also using an (implicitly) imported `List` type.
aliases.type: { references : FastDict.Dict ( Elm.Syntax.ModuleName.ModuleName, String.String ) Elm.Syntax.ModuleName.ModuleName, typeConstructs : FastDict.Dict ( Elm.Syntax.ModuleName.ModuleName, String.String ) Elm.Syntax.ModuleName.ModuleName, keepOperatorIsExposedFromParserAdvanced : Basics.Bool, ignoreOperatorIsExposedFromParserAdvanced : Basics.Bool }

aliases.name: ModuleTypes
aliases.comment:
Known types of members in a module.
Create with [`moduleDeclarationsToTypes`](#moduleDeclarationsToTypes)
and [`moduleInterfaceToTypes`](#moduleInterfaceToTypes)
aliases.type: { signatures : FastDict.Dict String.String (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Type String.String), typeAliases : FastDict.Dict String.String { parameters : List.List String.String, type_ : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Type String.String, recordFieldOrder : Maybe.Maybe (List.List String.String) }, choiceTypes : FastDict.Dict String.String { parameters : List.List String.String, variants : FastDict.Dict String.String (List.List (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Type String.String)) } }

aliases.name: TypedNode
aliases.comment:
A part in the syntax tree with an attached

  - [range](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/stil4m/elm-syntax/latest/Elm-Syntax-Range#Range) in the source
  - global [`Type`](#Type): concretely inferred,
    influenced by other branches and value/function (let) declaration type annotations
aliases.type: { range : Elm.Syntax.Range.Range, value : value, type_ : type_ }

values.name: elmCoreTypes
values.comment:
[`ModuleTypes`](#ModuleTypes) exposed in `elm/core`.

Please _always_ start with [`elmCoreTypes`](#elmCoreTypes)
and add further module info with [`FastDict.union`](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/miniBill/elm-fast-dict/latest/FastDict#union)
using [`moduleDeclarationsToTypes`](#moduleDeclarationsToTypes)
and [`moduleInterfaceToTypes`](#moduleInterfaceToTypes)

And if for some reason you already know used dependencies at compile time,
you can re-use the [code generator used for these elm/core types](https://github.com/lue-bird/elm-syntax-type-infer/tree/main/codegen)

values.name: importsToModuleOriginLookup
values.comment:
Calculate valid mappings of qualifications + name
to origin module based on a module's imports.

Requires all exposed [`ModuleTypes`](#ModuleTypes)
so we can resolve `exposing (..)` and `ChoiceType(..)`.
values.type: FastDict.Dict Elm.Syntax.ModuleName.ModuleName ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.ModuleTypes -> List.List (Elm.Syntax.Node.Node Elm.Syntax.Import.Import) -> ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.ModuleOriginLookup

values.name: moduleDeclarationsToTypes
values.comment:
Extract all known types
from declarations within a module.
Un-annotated value/function declarations are ignored.

For dependency modules, use [`moduleInterfaceToTypes`](#moduleInterfaceToTypes)
values.type: ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.ModuleOriginLookup -> List.List Elm.Syntax.Declaration.Declaration -> { types : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.ModuleTypes, errors : List.List String.String }

values.name: moduleInterfaceToTypes
values.comment:
Extract all known types
from module [metadata](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/elm/project-metadata-utils/latest/),
usually from parsing the `docs.json` of a dependency.

For elm-syntax modules, use [`moduleDeclarationsToTypes`](#moduleDeclarationsToTypes)
values.type: Elm.Docs.Module -> { types : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.ModuleTypes, errors : List.List String.String }

values.name: valueAndFunctionDeclarations
values.comment:
Infer types of
value/[`Elm.Syntax.Expression.Function`](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/stil4m/elm-syntax/latest/Elm-Syntax-Expression#Function) declarations
in a module.

    import Elm.Syntax.Node
    import Elm.Syntax.Expression
    import ElmSyntaxTypeInfer


    [ { declaration =
            Elm.Syntax.Node.empty
                { name = Elm.Syntax.Node.empty "hello"
                , expression =
                    Elm.Syntax.Node.empty
                        (Elm.Syntax.Expression.Literal "world")
                , arguments = []
                }
      , signature = Nothing
      , documentation = Nothing
      }
    ]
        |> ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.valueAndFunctionDeclarations
            { importedTypes = ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.elmCoreTypes
            , moduleOriginLookup = exampleModuleOriginLookup
            , otherModuleDeclaredTypes =
                []
                    |> ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.moduleDeclarationsToTypes
                        exampleModuleOriginLookup
                    |> .types
            }
    -->
    Ok
        (FastDict.singleton "hello"
            { type_ =
                ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypeNotVariable
                    (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypeConstruct
                        { moduleOrigin = [ "String" ], name = "String", arguments = [] }
                    )
            ...
            }
        )


    exampleModuleOriginLookup : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.ModuleOriginLookup
    exampleModuleOriginLookup =
        []
            |> ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.importsToModuleOriginLookup
                ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.elmCoreTypes
    
See [`ModuleTypes`](#ModuleTypes) and [`ModuleOriginLookup`](#ModuleOriginLookup)

Warning! Always first verify that provided type alias declarations aren't (mutually) recursive.
Otherwise this function might never terminate.
values.type: { importedTypes : FastDict.Dict Elm.Syntax.ModuleName.ModuleName ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.ModuleTypes, moduleOriginLookup : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.ModuleOriginLookup, otherModuleDeclaredTypes : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.ModuleTypes } -> List.List Elm.Syntax.Expression.Function -> Result.Result String.String (FastDict.Dict String.String { parameters : List.List (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Pattern (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Type String.String)) (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Type String.String)), result : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.TypedNode (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Expression (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Type String.String)) (ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Type String.String), type_ : ElmSyntaxTypeInfer.Type String.String, nameRange : Elm.Syntax.Range.Range, documentation : Maybe.Maybe { content : String.String, range : Elm.Syntax.Range.Range }, signature : Maybe.Maybe { range : Elm.Syntax.Range.Range, nameRange : Elm.Syntax.Range.Range, annotationTypeRange : Elm.Syntax.Range.Range, annotationType : Elm.Syntax.TypeAnnotation.TypeAnnotation } })
